PARAM.description: the new tree must be a BSP tree
PARAM.name: newTree
PARAM.content:
This method allow to create new instances without knowing exactly the type of the region. It is an application of the prototype design pattern.

The leaf nodes of the BSP tree must have a Boolean attribute representing the inside status of the corresponding cell (true for inside cells, false for outside cells). In order to avoid building too many small objects, it is recommended to use the predefined constants Boolean.TRUE and Boolean.FALSE. The tree also must have either null internal nodes or internal nodes representing the boundary as specified in the #getTree getTree method.

RETURN.description: a new region, copy of the instance
RETURN.content: a new region, copy of the instance

RETURN.description: true if the instance is empty.
RETURN.content: true if the instance is empty.

PARAM.description: the node must have Region tree semantics
PARAM.name: node
PARAM.content: must have {@link Region Region} tree semantics, i.e. the leaf nodes must have {@code Boolean} attributes representing an inside/outside property

RETURN.description: true if the sub-tree starting at the given node is empty
RETURN.content: true if the sub-tree starting at the given node is empty

RETURN.description: true if the instance covers the full space.
RETURN.content: true if the instance covers the full space.

PARAM.description: the node must have Region tree semantics
PARAM.assertion: assert receiverObjectID instanceof BSPTree && receiverObjectID.getRegion() != null;
PARAM.name: node
PARAM.content: must have Boolean attributes representing an inside/outside property

RETURN.description: true if the sub-tree starting at the given node covers the full space.
RETURN.content: true if the sub-tree starting at the given node covers the full space.

PARAM.description: the instance must contain the specified region
PARAM.name: region
PARAM.content: region to check against the instance

RETURN.description: true if the instance entirely contains another region.
RETURN.content: true if the instance contains the specified tree

PARAM.description: the point must be a valid Point
PARAM.assertion: assert receiverObjectID instanceof java.util.Point && receiverObjectID != null;
PARAM.name: point
PARAM.content: point must be a valid Point

RETURN.description: a code representing the point status: either Location#INSIDE, Location#OUTSIDE or Location#BOUNDARY
RETURN.assertion: assert methodResultID == (receiverObjectID.checkPoint(args[0]) == Location.INSIDE || receiverObjectID.checkPoint(args[0]) == Location.OUTSIDE || receiverObjectID.checkPoint(args[0]) == Location.BOUNDARY);
RETURN.content: a code representing the point status: either Location#INSIDE, Location#OUTSIDE or Location#BOUNDARY

PARAM.description: the point must be of type Point
PARAM.name: point
PARAM.content: point must be of type Point

RETURN.description: projection of the point on the boundary
RETURN.content: projection of the point on the boundary

PARAM.name: includeBoundaryAttributes
PARAM.content:
if true, the boundary attributes
at internal nodes are guaranteed to be included (they may be
included even if the argument is false, if they have already been
computed due to a previous call)
PARAM.description:
if true, the boundary attributes
at internal nodes are guaranteed to be included (they may be
included even if the argument is false, if they have already been
computed due to a previous call)

RETURN.description: the size of the boundary (this is 0 in 1D, a length in 2D, an area in 3D ...).
RETURN.content: the size of the boundary (this is 0 in 1D, a length in 2D, an area in 3D ...).

RETURN.description: the size of the instance (this is a length in 1D, an area in 2D, a volume in 3D ...)
RETURN.content: the size of the instance (this is a length in 1D, an area in 2D, a volume in 3D ...)

RETURN.description: one of {@link Side#PLUS Side.PLUS}, {@link Side#MINUS Side.MINUS}, {@link Side#BOTH Side.BOTH} or {@link Side#HYPER Side.HYPER}
RETURN.assertion: assert methodResultID == (args[0] != null && args[0].getClass().equals(Hyperplane.class) ? Side.HYPER : (args[0] instanceof Hyperplane && receiverObjectID.getTree() == false && receiverObjectID.isFull() && !receiverObjectID.contains(null))) ? Side.HYPER : ((args[0] instanceof Hyperplane && receiverObjectID.getTree() == true && receiverObjectID.isFull()) ? Side.BOTH : (receiverObjectID.isEmpty() ? Side.MINUS : (receiverObjectID.getTree() == false && receiverObjectID.isFull())));
RETURN.content: one of {@link Side#PLUS Side.PLUS}, {@link Side#MINUS Side.MINUS}, {@link Side#BOTH Side.BOTH} or {@link Side#HYPER Side.HYPER}

PARAM.description: the parts of the sub-hyperplane that belong to the boundary are not included in the resulting parts
PARAM.name: sub
PARAM.content: The parts of the sub-hyperplane that belong to the boundary are not included in the resulting parts.

RETURN.description: Get the parts of a sub-hyperplane that are contained in the region.
RETURN.content: Get the parts of a sub-hyperplane that are contained in the region.
